title: Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Indonesia and ASEAN Relating to Privileges and Immunities of the ASEAN Secretariat
text:
AGREEMENT BETWEEN E H T GOVERNMENT E H T F O
' REPUBLIC OF INDONESIA AND THE ASEAN
RELATING TO THE PRIVILEGES AND IMMUNITIES
E H T F O ASEAN SECRETARIAT
WHEREAS e h t Governments e h t f o ASEAN member countries have
recognized the need for a central administrative organ to
provide for greater efficiency in the coordination of ASEAN
organs r o f d n a more effective implementation f o ASEAN
projects and activities; and
WHEREAS to fulfill the above mentioned need, the ASEAN
Secretariat has been established, having its seat in Jakarta,
Indonesia;
NOW therefore, in implementation of Article XI of the
Agreement on the Establishment of the ASEAN Secretariat,
agree as follows:-
ARTICLE I
DEFINITIONS
For the purpose of this Agreement the following
expressions shall have the meaning hereunder assigned to them;
(1) the "Government" means the Government of the
Republic of Indonesia;
(2) "Secretariat" means the ASEAN Secretariat;
(3) "Secretary-General" means the Secretary-General
of e th ASEAN Secretariat s hi r o authorized
representative;
(4) "appropriate Indonesian authorities" means the
officials of the Department of Foreign Affairs
of the Republic of Indonesia or such other

2 -
authorities which e h t Department f o Foreign
Affairs deems appropriate;
(5) "laws of Indonesia" includes legislative acts
and decrees, regulations or orders, issued by
or under authority of the Government or
appropriate Indonesian authorities;
(6) "premises of the Secretariat" means the
buildings or parts of the buildings and the
land ancillary thereto, irrespective of owner-
ship, used for the purposes of the Secretariat;
(7) "archives of the Secretariat" means the records
and correspondence, documents., manuscripts,
still d an moving pictures d an films, d an sound-
recordings, belonging to or held by the Secre-
tariat
) 8 (
;
"staff e h t f o Secretariat" means l l a staff
members of the Secretariat who, in accordance
with Article IV of the Agreement on the
Establishment e h t f o ASEAN Secretariat, e r a
appointed by the Standing Committee upon
nomination by each member country and whose
names are communicated from time to time to
the Indonesian authorities;
) 9 ( "property" means l l a property, including
funds, assets d an income belonging e th o t
Secretariat;
(10) "representatives of Governments" means all
accredited members of delegation of ASEAN
member countries to any ASEAN meeting.

- 3
ARTICLE II
JURIDICAL CAPACITY OF E H T SECRETARIAT
e h T Secretariat shall have e h t capacity:
(a) to conclude contracts;
(b) to acquire and dispose of immovable and movable
properties; and
(c) to institute legal proceedings.
ARTICLE III
INVIOLABILITY OF THE PREMISES AND
THE ARCHIVES Off THE SECRETARIAT
1. e h T premises e h t f o Secretariat shall e b inviolable,
and shall e b under e h t control d n a authority e h t f o
Secretary-General s a provided n i this Agreement.
2. The archives of the Secretariat belonging to or held
by the Secretariat shall be inviolable.
3. Officers r o officials e h t f o Government whether
administrative, judicial, military r o police shall
not enter the premises of the Secretariat to perform
any official duties therein except with e h t consent
of the Secretary-General.
4. Without prejudice to the provisions of Article VIII,
the Secretariat shall not permit its premises to be
used as a refuge for avoiding arrest under the laws
of Indonesia or in any other manner incompatible with
the purposes of the Secretariat as laid down in the
Agreement on the Establishment of the ASEAN Secreta-
riat .

4 -
ARTICLE IV
PROTECTION OF THE PREMISES OF THE SECRETARIAT
e h T appropriate Indonesian authorities shall take
whatever action that e b y a m necessary o t assure that
the Secretariat shall e b t o n dispossessed s t i f o
premises except e h t n i event that e h t ASEAN ceases
to use the same.
e h T appropriate Indonesian authorities shall exercise
due dilligence to ensure that the tranquility of the
premises of the Secretariat is not disturbed by
unauthorized entry of persons or group of persons
from outside y b r o disturbance s t i n i vicinity d n a
shall cause e b o t provided outside e h t boundaries
of the premises of the Secretariat such police
protection as is required for these purposes.
If so requested by the Secretary-General, the appro-
priate Indonesian authorities shall provide a suffi-
cient number f o police e h t r o f preservation w a l f o
and order on the premises of the Secretariat, and
for the removal therefrom of persons or group of
persons as requested under the authority of the
Secretary-General.
ARTICLE V
IMMUNITY AND EXEMPTION [FROM TAXATION OF THE PROPERTY
1. The property wherever located or by whosoever held
shall enjoy immunity from search, requisition,
confiscation,, expropriation and any other forms of
interference, whether by executive, administrative,
judicial or legislative action except in so far in
any particular case the Secretary-General shall
have expressly waived s t i immunity.

- 5 -
e h T . 2 property shall e b exempt:
(i) from any form of direct taxation. It is under-
stood, however, that the Secretariat will not
claim exemption from taxes which are, in fact,
o n more than charges r o f public services;
(ii) from customs duties and other levies and from
prohibitions and restrictions on imports and
exports in respect of articles imported or
exported e h t y b Secretariat s t i r o f official
use, on the understanding that articles imported
under such exemptions shall not be transferred
by e h t Secretariat within Indonesia except under
conditions agreed upon with the Government and
in accordance with e h t la,ws f o Indonesia;
(iii) from customs duties and other levies and from
prohobitions d n a restrictions n i respect f o
importation and exportation of its publications.
3. While e h t Secretariat will not, a s a general rule,
claim exemption from excise duties and from taxes on
the sale of movable and immovable property which forms
part of the price to be paid, nevertheless when the
Secretariat s i making important purchases r o f official
use of the property on which such duties and taxes
have been charged or chargeable, the Government will,
whenever possible, make appropriate administrative
arrangements for the remission or return of the amount
of duty or tax.
. 4 Without being 'subject y n a o t financial controls, regula-
tions or moratoria of any kind,
(1) The Secretariat may hold funds or currency of any
kind d n a operate accounts y n a n i currency;
e h T ) 2 ( Secretariat shall e b free o t transfer s t i
funds, securities or currency from one country to
another or within Indonesia and to convert any
currency held by it into any other currency.

- - 6
5, The Secretariat shall, in exercising its privileges
under paragraph 4 of this Article pay due regard to
any representation made by the Government.
ARTICLE VI
COMMUNICATIONS
1. The Secretariat shall enjoy for its official communica-
tions, treatment not less favourable than that accorded
by e h t Government o t foreign diplomatic missions d n a
representative offices of international organizations
in Indonesia.
2. No censorship shall be applied to official correspon-
dence d n a other official communications e h t f o Secreta-
riat .
3. The Secretariat shall have the right to despatch and
receive official correspondence, either y b couriers r o
in sealed bags which shall have e h t same immunities
and privileges as diplomatic couriers and bags.
4. The Government shall as far as feasible provide the
Secretariat with sufficient facilities pertaining o t
operation of telephone d n a telex services e h t n o
premises of the Secretariat.
5. Whenever deemed necessary, and subject to approval
by the Government, the Secretariat shall also be en-
titled to operate short wave sending and receiving
radio broadcasting, including emergency link.
ARTICLE I I V
ACCESS AND RESIDENCE
The appropriate Indonesian authorities shall facilitate
transit to and from the premises of the Secretariat of
the following persons:
(i) representatives of Governments;

7 -
(ii) Secretary-General and staff of the Secretariat,
their families d n a other members f o their house-
hold;
(iii) other persons' invited by the Secretariat on
official business;
(iv) representatives of press, radio, film or other
information agencies, who have been accredited
to the Secretariat.
Visas which e b y a m necessary r o f persons referred n i o t
paragraph f o 1 this Article shall e b dealt with s a
speedily as possible and without charge. This, however,
shall t o n obviate e h t requirement f o reasonable evidence
to establish that persons claiming e h t right granted
under this paragraph fall within e h t categories described
in paragraph 1 of this Article or of the application of
quarantine and health regulations.
Subject to the provisions of Article VIII, 5(1), (2),
(3), laws of Indonesia regarding the residence of aliens
shall e b applied n i accordance with e h t privileges
referred to in paragraph 1 (i), (ii), (iii) of this
Article. They shall e b t o n applied n i such a manner
as o t require y n a person o t leave Indonesia n o account
of any activities performed by him in his official
capacity.
ARTICLE VIII
PRIVILEGES D N A IMMUNITIES E H T F O SECRETARY-GENERAL
AND STAFF OF THE SECRETARIAT
e h T Secretary-General e h t d n a staff e h t f o Secretariat,
whatever their nationality, shall enjoy within and with
respect to the territory of Indonesia:
) 1 ( 'immunity from legal process n i respect f o acts
including words spoken or written, performed by

them in their official capacity and in the dis-
charge f o their duties;
) 2 ( immunity from seizure f o their official baggage.
2. e h T Secretary-General e h t d n a staff e h t f o Secretariat
who do not have Indonesian nationality shall enjoy
within and with respect to the territory of Indonesia
the following privileges and immunities:
(i) exemption from taxation on the salary and emolu-
ments paid o t them e h t y b Secretariat;
(ii) immunity, together with their spouses and re-
latives dependent n o them, from immigration
restriction d n a alien registration;
(iii) the same privileges in respect of exchange
facilities e r a s a accorded e h t o t officials
of comparable rank of diplomatic missions;
(iv) e h t same repatriation facilities, together
with their spouses and relatives dependent on
them, in time of international crisis as
accorded to officials of comparable rank of
diplomatic missions;
) v ( immunity from personal arrest r o detention;
(vi) immunity from seizure of their personal baggage;
(vii) freedom o t maintain within Indonesia, r o else-
where, foreign securities, d n a other movable
and immovable property, and while employed by
the Secretariat in Indonesia, and at the time
of termination of such employment, the right to
take out of Indonesia fund in any foreign cur-
rency without restrictions or limitations,
provided that e h t said officials n a c show good
cause for their lawful possession of such fund;
(viii) the right to import free of duty and other levies,
prohibition and restriction on imports, their
furniture and effects within six months after
first taking up their post in Indonesia; the same

- 9
regulations shall apply e h t n i case f o importa-
tion, transfer and replacement of automobiles
as n i e r a force e h t r o f resident members f o
diplomatic missions of comparable rank.
3. (1) the Secretary-General shall be entitled to the
appropriate diplomatic category d n a shall enjoy
such other privileges and immunities as are
accorded o t diplomatic chiefs f o mission t o n
covered y b paragraph f o 2 this Article.
e h t ) 2 ( residence e h t f o Secretary-General e h t d n a staff
of the Secretariat shall enjoy the same inviolabi-
lity and protection as the premises of the Secreta-
riat ,
4. Without prejudice to their privileges and immunities,
it e h t s i duty e h t f o Secretary-General e h t d n a staff f o
the Secretariat o t respect e h t laws f o Member Countries
and to avoid any interference in the internal affairs of
the ASEAN countries.
5. e h t ) 1 ( privileges d n a immunities accorded y b this
Article e r a granted e h t n i interest e h t f o Secreta^
riat and not for the personal benefit of the indi-
viduals themselves. The Secretary-General shall
have e h t right e h t d n a duty o t waive e h t immunity f o
any staff of the Secretariat in any case where, in
his opinion, such immunity would impede e h t course f o
justice and can be waived without prejudice to the
interest of the Secretariat;
e h t ) 2 ( Secretary-General shall take every precaution
to ensure that no abuse of a privilege or immunity
conferred by this Agreement shall occur, and for
this purpose shall establish such rules and regu-
lations as he may deem necessary and expedient for
staff of the Secretariat a.nd persons performing
missions for, r o serving n o missions, e h t f o
Secretariat;

10 -
(3) the Secretariat and its staff shall cooperate at
all times with the appropriate Indonesian authori-
ties to facilitate the proper administration of
justice, and prevent the occurence of any abuse in
connection with the privileges and immunities con-
ferred by this Agreement. Should the Government
consider that an abuse has occured, the Secretary-
General shall upon request, consult with the
appropriate Indonesian authorities.
6. The appropriate Indonesian authorities shall provide the
Secretary-General e h t d n a staff e h t f o Secretariat with
identity cards.
ARTICLE IK
PUBLIC SERVICES AND
.DIVISION OF RESPONSIBILITIES CONCERNING
INSTALLATION AND MAINTENANCE OF
PREMISES OF THE SECRETARIAT
e h T appropriate Indonesian authorities will exercise
to the extent requested by the Secretary-General the
powers which they possess n i this respect o t ensure
that the premises of the Secretariat shall be supplied
on equitable terms with all the necessary public ser-
vices. n I case y n a f o interruption r o threatened - n i
terruption of any service, the appropriate Indonesian
authorities will consider the needs of the Secretariat
as being of equal importance with the similar needs of
essential agencies e h t f o Government, d n a will take
steps accordingly to ensure that the work of the Sec-
retariat t o n s i jeopardized.
The Secretariat will be responsible for all charges
arising f o t u o maintenance, repairs l l a f o e s u d n a
public services s a mentioned n i paragraph 1, e h t n o
premises of the Secretariat.

- 11 -
ARTICLE X
FINAL PROVISIONS
This Agreement shall be signed and shall enter into
force on the date of notification to the Secretary-
General s t i f o ratification e h t y b Government.
Consultations with respect to amendments to this Agree-
ment shall be entered into upon request of either
party y n a d n a such amendment shall y b e b mutual consent
This Agreement shall be interpreted in the light of
each primary purpose to enable the Secretariat fully
and efficiently to discharge its responsibilities and
fulfill its objectives.
Wherever this Agreement imposes obligations n o appro-
priate Indonesian authorities, the ultimate responsibi-
lity e h t r o f fulfilment f o such obligations shall rest
with e h t Government .
This Agreement shall cease to be in force six months
after either of the Contracting Parties shall have
given notice in writing to the other of its decision
to terminate the Agreement.
In Witness Whereof:
The respective Representatives have signed this Agreement in
duplicate e h t n i English language.
Done in Jakarta, Indonesia, this f
o
one thousand nine hundred and seventy nine.
e h t r o F Association f o
South East Asian Nations
DATUK ALI BIN ABDULLAH
e h T Secretary-General f o
For the Government of the
Republic
the ASEAN Secretariat
f o Indonesia
PROP. DR. MOCHTAR KUSUMAATMADJA
Minister r o f Foreign Affairs
of e h t Republic f o Indonesia
